title: Folklore Conjecture on γ_d
context_text: Here $\gamma_d = \lim_{n\to\infty} g(n,d)/2^n$, where $g(n,d)$ is the smallest cardinality of a subset of vertices of the $n$-cube intersecting every $d$-subcube. This conjecture (see [3]) was refuted by Ellis, Ivan and Leader [4], who proved $(\gamma_d)^{1/d}\leq 2^{-1/8+o(1)}$; the present paper sharpens that refutation.
paper_contribution: Proves that $(\gamma_d)^{1/d}\leq 2^{-1/2+o(1)}$, substantially improving the bound of Ellis, Ivan and Leader [4] that originally refuted the conjecture.
notes: The conjecture is already refuted; this paper contributes a sharper quantitative bound on γ_d.
paper_title: Erasure codes and Turán hypercube problems

title: Conjecture on L(d)
context_text: Here $L(d)$ denotes the smallest $L$ such that there exists $\varepsilon=\varepsilon(d)>0$ with the property that for every $n$ there is a $(d,L,n)$-code of cardinality at least $\varepsilon 2^n$. The conjectured value equals the upper bound that comes from a natural combinatorial construction, and equality holds for $d\leq 3$.
paper_contribution: Builds on the refutation by Ellis, Ivan and Leader [4], who showed $L(d)\leq\frac{5}{6}\binom{d}{\lfloor d/2\rfloor}$ for $d\geq 4$; the paper analyzes their construction further and obtains improved bounds via Turán density arguments for hypergraph augmentations.
notes: The conjecture was refuted by [4] before this paper appeared; Alon's contribution is quantitative improvement rather than a new disproof.
paper_title: Erasure codes and Turán hypercube problems
